description:
## What
Implement GET /api/v1/trends/tags, GET /api/v1/trends/statuses, and GET /api/v1/trends/links as stubs that return 200 with an empty JSON array []. These endpoints are unimplemented in v1 but clients call them and break with 404 responses.

## Why
Phanpy, Elk, and Ivory all call the trends endpoints on their explore/discover pages. A 404 causes some clients to show error states or crash the explore view. Returning [] is the correct stub behavior for a small server. Part of sub-epic 8-C.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: test Phanpy behavior with trends returning [] vs 404 by checking Phanpy source (github.com/cheeaun/phanpy) to find where it calls trends and how it handles errors; curl mastodon.social /api/v1/trends/tags and document the Tag object shape (for future implementation reference); check if any clients require a specific schema within the [] or accept truly empty.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add three axum routes for GET /api/v1/trends/tags, GET /api/v1/trends/statuses, GET /api/v1/trends/links; each handler returns Json(Vec::<serde_json::Value>::new()) with 200; requires Bearer token authentication (use existing auth extractor); add limit param parsing (accept but ignore — return [] regardless).
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write three short integration tests; one per endpoint; assert 200 status; assert body is exactly []; assert authenticated request succeeds; assert unauthenticated request returns 401 (consistent with other protected endpoints).

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/trends/tags response shape (for documentation); the stub correctness oracle is simply the JSON schema [] which is self-evident.
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite; authenticated GET to each trends URL; assert 200 and body == []; also assert 401 without Bearer token.

## Defensive programming checklist
- All three endpoints require authentication (Bearer token) — do not make them public
- Return Content-Type: application/json with the empty array
- Accept and ignore limit, offset, and other params without erroring
- No .unwrap() anywhere
- Do not return null — must be [] (empty JSON array, not JSON null)
title: GET /api/v1/trends/* — return empty-array stubs
